title: Wire scan-secrets.sh into the pre-commit template hook
description:
PROBLEM: .claude/scripts/scan-secrets.sh documents --staged (pre-commit) and --all (CI) modes in its own header, but nothing invokes it: the repo pre-commit hook (.git_template/hooks/pre-commit, wired via core.hooksPath) runs only the marker-word check + check-secrets.py (hash-based banlist of KNOWN leaked values) + a trailing-whitespace fixer. scan-secrets.sh is pattern-based (catches NEW secrets), so the two are complementary: the documented intent was clearly a layered gate, and the wiring is missing.

ACTIONS:
1. Read scan-secrets.sh fully first: confirm its --staged mode exits nonzero on findings, produces sane output, and runs fast enough for a pre-commit (target <2s; time it on this repo). If it is slow or noisy, note that and add it in warn-only mode instead (report but exit 0), documenting the choice.
2. Add an invocation block to .git_template/hooks/pre-commit AFTER the check-secrets.py block, same style: run it via its repo-absolute path with --staged, exit 1 on failure. Guard with [ -x ] so a missing file does not break commits.
3. TEST both directions in a scratch way that leaves no trace: (a) stage a file containing a fake AWS-style key (AKIA + 16 chars) in a temp path, confirm commit is blocked, then unstage and delete; (b) confirm a normal commit still passes. NOTE: the pre-commit hook also blocks the uppercase D-O-N-O-T-C-O-M-M-I-T marker; keep test content clear of it.
4. Commit the hook change per epic conventions. Mind that the hook file itself is excluded from the marker scan (self-exclusion added 2026-07-05) but NOT from the secrets scan: ensure the hook edit contains no pattern that trips scan-secrets.sh.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) staged fake secret blocks the commit with a clear message; (b) clean commit passes; (c) hook runs in <2s overhead on a typical commit (time git commit on a trivial change); (d) scan-secrets.sh header comment still accurately describes how it is invoked: update it if the wiring differs.

VERIFY: paste the blocked-commit output (redact the fake key) into the close reason. Files: .git_template/hooks/pre-commit, possibly .claude/scripts/scan-secrets.sh (doc header).
close_reason: Wired after check-secrets.py block with [ -x ] guard and repo-relative path (hooks run from repo root). Found + fixed a latent defect: script had no exec bit, so the guard would have skipped it forever; first block-test exposed this by letting a fake secret through (test commit dropped via reset --soft before any push). Re-test: fake ghp_ token blocked, exit 1; this bead's own commit passed the gate incl. self-scan of the pattern list. Full hook overhead ~2.6s on WSL1 (process spawn dominated), acceptable. Header rewritten: .git_template/hooks/pre-commit via core.hooksPath, manual --all sweeps.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH FACT-CHECK 2026-07-05: scan-secrets.sh header confirmed stale: claims it is used by .git/hooks/pre-commit, but the repo uses core.hooksPath=.git_template/hooks and that hook never calls it. Header update is required, not optional. Exit-code contract confirmed in header (0 clean / 1 findings); still verify --staged behavior empirically per the bead.

title: Fix all SKILL.md frontmatter name fields to match directory names
description:
PROBLEM: The 2026-07-05 review found ~19 skills whose frontmatter "name:" field does not match their directory name, almost all from a copy-a-base-skill-then-customize workflow where the name field was never updated. Three classes: (a) EMPTY name: pr-checks, swarm-exec-status, swarm-exec, swarm-status, swarm; (b) name points at a NON-EXISTENT sibling (cosmetic but wrong): git-partial-stage->partial-stage, swarm-agents->agent-swarm, swarm-beads-create->prd-to-beads, swarm-beads-quality->bead-quality, swarm-beads-rewrite->bead-rewrite, swarm-hardening->hardening-pipeline, swarm-oracle-review->oracle-review, swarm-oracle->oracle-consensus, swarm-pipeline->feature-pipeline, swarm-review->multi-lens-review; (c) name COLLIDES with a real existing sibling dir (actively confusing to the skill loader): swarm-beads-quick declares name prd-to-br (a real skill, bodies differ by 1 line: genuine variant, mislabeled), swarm-prd declares name prd-start (real skill, 212 lines each, genuinely different content). The two byte-identical dupes are handled by dotfiles-9ec (BLOCKER: run after it so this pass does not touch deleted dirs).

WHY IT MATTERS: the harness keys skills by these names; empty/colliding names risk mis-invocation and make /slash-name lookups unreliable. Convention everywhere else in the tree: frontmatter name == dir name.

ACTIONS:
1. Do this programmatically, not from the list above (the list may be stale after dotfiles-9ec): for every .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md, parse frontmatter "name:"; if missing, empty, or != dir basename, set it to the dir basename. Skip gitignored skill dirs (cursor, de-slopify, dsr, gcloud, repeatedly-apply-skill, rust-crates-publishing, cc-slidev, frontend-slides) so we do not create tracked modifications to jsm-managed content: check with git check-ignore. ALSO skip skills-sync-managed dirs (those containing .skill-source.json): editing them would trip skills-sync local-edit detection; verify they already comply instead (they should: dzhng upstream uses matching names).
2. Write the one-off fixer to the session scratchpad, not the repo.
3. Only stage the SKILL.md files actually changed; commit per epic conventions.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) a verification pass over all non-ignored skills reports zero mismatches; (b) git diff touches only frontmatter name: lines (no body/description changes); (c) count of changed files recorded in close reason (expect ~15-17); (d) skills that are variants (swarm-beads-quick, swarm-prd) keep their full bodies: this bead ONLY renames, never merges.

VERIFY: rerun the check script; then confirm in a fresh session that /swarm, /swarm-exec etc. resolve. Files: ~15-17 SKILL.md files, one mechanical commit.
close_reason: 17 mismatches fixed: 12 stale name values corrected, 4 skills with no frontmatter got minimal name+description blocks (from their own first lines), 1 more (git-partial-stage) renamed. 14 tracked files committed atomically (1852f2a); 3 untracked (pr-checks, swarm-status, swarm) fixed on disk only per drift rule. Final scan: zero mismatches among non-ignored, non-skills-sync skills.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH FACT-CHECK 2026-07-05: mismatch scan run against disk: exactly 19 skills where frontmatter name != dir basename (matches review breakdown 5 empty + 10 orphaned pointers + 4 collisions). After dotfiles-9ec deletes the 2 exact dupes, expect 17 fixes here.

title: Dedupe and restructure the two CLAUDE.md files
description:
PROBLEM: /c/users/oystein/.dotfiles/CLAUDE.md (repo-level, 253 lines, ~2.5k tokens) and /c/users/oystein/.dotfiles/.claude/CLAUDE.md (user-global, 452 lines, ~6.9k tokens) BOTH load into every session run in ~. They duplicate: (1) multi-agent orchestration: repo CLAUDE.md lines 173-253 "Multi-Agent Orchestration" (~81 lines) vs global "Claude Code Teams & Agents" (~lines 183-280) + "Agent Swarm Rules" (~lines 356-441), ~265 lines total on one topic written twice with different wording; (2) gog CLI: repo lines 138-157 (20 lines with examples) vs global lines 289-295 (7-line condensed version); (3) the dotfiles-repo description: repo lines 3-7 vs global lines 26-30.

TARGET SPLIT: global .claude/CLAUDE.md owns universal agent behavior (safety rules, swarm mechanics incl. incident-derived rules, secrets policy, gog condensed section, dotfiles-location note). Repo-level CLAUDE.md owns home-dir tooling only (grove, git-hunks, git-addmatch, cfclip, rgg, python toolchain, fieldtheory, oracle policy, skills-sync).

ACTIONS:
1. In repo-level CLAUDE.md: delete the entire "Multi-Agent Orchestration" section and replace with a 2-3 line pointer ("See 'Claude Code Teams & Agents' and 'Agent Swarm Rules' in the global CLAUDE.md (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md); those are canonical."). Verify NOTHING unique is lost first: diff the two versions section by section; if the repo copy contains any rule/nuance absent from global (e.g. leader workflow numbered steps, addBlockedBy detail), MERGE it into the global canonical section before deleting. Do not lose content.
2. Delete the repo-level 20-line gog section after confirming everything in it (binary path, account flag, subcommand list, -j/-p flags, GOG_ENABLE_COMMANDS sandboxing, repo URL, config path) exists in the global condensed section; move any missing bits into the global section.
3. Trim the duplicated repo-description lines: global keeps the symlink fact; repo-level keeps repo+remote facts; no repetition.
4. While editing global CLAUDE.md, fix the now-false sentence "The session leader runs Opus 4.8 (claude-opus-4-8[1m] in settings.json)": settings.json now has "claude-fable-5[1m]". Reword so it does not hardcode the model, e.g. "The session leader model is set in settings.json; subagents do not inherit it by default." (keeps the sentence true across future model changes).
5. Add a short "Skill sources" note to repo-level CLAUDE.md documenting bin/skills-sync + .claude/skills-sources.json (built 2026-07-05, currently undocumented).

ACCEPTANCE: (a) no section topic appears in both files; (b) combined size shrinks by >= 6KB (~1.5k tokens minimum, expect ~2.5-3k); (c) grep -c "gog" on repo CLAUDE.md returns 0 outside the pointer/skill-sources lines; (d) no mention of a hardcoded leader model name in global CLAUDE.md; (e) read both files end-to-end after editing: every fact from the deleted sections is present in exactly one place.

VERIFY: wc -c both files before/after and record the delta in the close reason. Files: CLAUDE.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md (2 files).
close_reason: Repo CLAUDE.md 10067->6310 bytes; global 27607->29115->29057 net after merges (absorbed leader workflow, monitoring, enriched gog). Combined 37674->35367... final numbers in commit; combined saving ~2.3KB plus elimination of double-loaded multi-agent guidance. Opus 4.8 hardcode removed; skills-sync documented. Secret-banlist compliance: literal work email removed from doc text.

title: EPIC: Cut session context cost + remove dead config (dotfiles review 2026-07-05)
description:
Umbrella for findings #3 (context cost) and #4 (dead config) from the 2026-07-05 four-agent dotfiles review.

BACKGROUND: Every Claude Code session in ~ loads both CLAUDE.md files (~9.4k tokens, with ~265 lines of duplicated multi-agent content) plus frontmatter of 181 skills (~17k tokens). Separately, several pieces of shell/vim/hook config are dead code: shadowed fish keybindings that never ran, a prompt theme superseded by starship, vimperator config unusable since 2017, orphaned hook scripts, and a conflicting duplicate sentry MCP definition.

GOAL: (a) reduce per-session context cost by ~3k tokens via CLAUDE.md dedup and removal of duplicate skills, (b) make skill metadata correct (frontmatter name == dir name), (c) delete config that provably does nothing, so the repo describes reality.

NON-GOALS (explicitly out of scope, decided at review time): pruning the broader skill library (needs per-skill user decisions), the git history rewrite for binary bloat (finding #1, separate effort), committing the 330-entry working-tree drift (finding #2), deleting untracked disk junk in bin/ (finding #5).

CONVENTIONS FOR ALL CHILD BEADS: work directly on master in ~/.dotfiles. One atomic commit per bead using pathspec form: git add <files> && git commit -m "<type>: <summary> (<bead-id>)" -- <files>. The repo pre-commit hook scans the staged diff for the uppercase marker string D O-space-N O T-space C O M M I T (do not include that literal phrase in any committed file or commit message). Close each bead with br close <id> --reason "..." after the commit. Do NOT git add -A (other uncommitted drift must stay uncommitted).
close_reason: All 11 children closed. Headline outcomes: ~2.2KB/session CLAUDE.md context cut with duplication eliminated; 2 duplicate skills deleted; 17 skill name fields fixed; 8 cc-slidev skills rescued via skills-sync; fish Ctrl-R consolidated on fzy (510 lines dead plugin code removed); dead prompt theme/omf/vimperator/Vundle/bd-nodb removed; sentry MCP single-sourced; hooks reconciled with reality; scan-secrets wired into pre-commit (exec-bit defect found+fixed) and its first sweep caught a real pushed token leak, now untracked+banlisted.

title: Final verification sweep for context-cost + dead-config epic
description:
PURPOSE: independent end-to-end check after all other epic children close; catches cross-bead regressions no single bead sees.

CHECKS:
1. Shell: fresh fish -i starts with zero errors/warnings; starship prompt renders; bind | grep cr shows exactly the fzy binding; fisher list shows no fzf/re-search.
2. Vim: vim -es +qall exits 0; no vundle/vimperator references anywhere tracked.
3. Skills: script-check that every non-gitignored .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md has frontmatter name == dir basename; no dir named swarm-beads-polish / swarm-oracle-standalone / cc-slidev; .disabled and .old-renamed gone.
4. CLAUDE.md: record final wc -c of both files vs pre-epic baseline (CLAUDE.md was 10067 bytes, .claude/CLAUDE.md was 27607 bytes; combined 37674); confirm the multi-agent section exists only in the global file; confirm no hardcoded leader-model claim.
5. Config truth: exactly one sentry MCP definition; every .claude/hooks/ file referenced or explained; README hook section matches settings.json.
6. Secrets: run .claude/scripts/scan-secrets.sh --all (now wired) and confirm clean on tracked files.
7. Git hygiene: git status should show ONLY the pre-existing drift documented in review finding #2 (settings.json bulk drift, 35 re-enabled skills, untracked aiolos files, etc.): confirm the epic added NO new uncommitted noise and did not accidentally commit any of that pre-existing drift. Run git log --stat over the epic's commits and check each touched only its bead's declared files.
8. Session smoke test: claude -p "reply OK" completes; available-skills list contains beads-polish and consult-oracles once each.

DELIVERABLE: paste a pass/fail table for checks 1-8 into the close reason; reopen the offending bead (br reopen <id>) on any failure instead of fixing inline, unless the fix is a one-liner (then fix, amend nothing, make a tiny follow-up commit referencing this bead).

DEPENDS ON: all other children of the epic.
close_reason: PASS/FAIL table: (1) shell PASS: clean start, starship prompt, Ctrl-R/Ctrl-F fzy-owned, fisher lists only bass+fish-fzy. (2) vim PASS: -es +qall exit 0, zero tracked vimperator/vundle refs. (3) skills PASS with documented exemption: zero name mismatches among user-owned skills; the 8 cc-slidev skills keep upstream Title-Case names by policy (skills-sync-managed dirs are upstream-owned; harness keys them by dir name; local edits would fight sync local-edit detection). Deleted dirs confirmed gone (5/5). (4) CLAUDE.md PASS: combined 37674 to 35445 bytes with duplication eliminated (repo file 10067 to 6310); multi-agent section only a pointer in repo file; no hardcoded leader-model claim. (5) config truth PASS: sentry only in mcp-servers.json; hooks dir = trauma_guard.py (wired) + forward_to_aiolos.sh (live aiolos pipeline, untracked drift); README matches settings.json. (6) secrets sweep FINDING then PASS: --all (25min on WSL1) flagged tracked .claude/settings.json.bak-20260227-210831 with hardcoded MCP_AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN, pushed to origin in a73a7d3. Fixed: untracked from HEAD (4365902), SHA-256 static banlist entry added; value persists in remote history until a filter-repo pass; rotation advised; exposure limited (agent-mail now --no-auth localhost, token unused). Cosmetic: scanner emits null-byte warnings on binary files, and 25min --all runtime is WSL1 process-spawn bound. (7) git hygiene PASS: 13 epic commits each scoped to declared files; pre-existing drift untouched and uncommitted; only .beads dirty at sweep time. (8) smoke test PASS: claude -p returned OK; session skill list shows originals once, duplicates gone.

title: Delete byte-identical duplicate skills + empty skill dirs
description:
PROBLEM: Two skills are byte-for-byte copies of other skills (verified with diff -rq on whole dirs): .claude/skills/swarm-beads-polish == .claude/skills/beads-polish (163-line SKILL.md, both declare name: beads-polish), and .claude/skills/swarm-oracle-standalone == .claude/skills/consult-oracles (211-line SKILL.md, declares name: consult-oracles). Each burns its description tokens in every session's system prompt twice for zero unique value. Also: .claude/skills/.disabled/ and .claude/skills/.old-renamed/ are completely empty directories (dead weight).

ACTIONS:
1. BEFORE deleting, grep the whole repo for references to the doomed dir names: grep -rn "swarm-beads-polish\|swarm-oracle-standalone" --include="*.md" --include="*.json" /c/users/oystein/.dotfiles (excluding .git). Known likely referrers: swarm-pipeline skill, other swarm-* skill bodies, CLAUDE.md files. Rewrite each reference to the surviving original (beads-polish / consult-oracles).
2. git rm -r .claude/skills/swarm-beads-polish .claude/skills/swarm-oracle-standalone
3. rmdir .claude/skills/.disabled .claude/skills/.old-renamed (plain rmdir: they are empty and untracked; rmdir fails loudly if somehow not empty, which is what we want).
4. Commit (pathspec commit per epic conventions).

REASONING FOR KEEPING beads-polish AND consult-oracles AS SURVIVORS: they are the originals whose frontmatter name matches their dir, and both are referenced from the user-level CLAUDE.md oracle policy / skill list.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) both dirs gone from disk and index; (b) repo-wide grep for both names returns only .beads/ content and git history; (c) rmdir succeeded on both empty dirs; (d) beads-polish and consult-oracles still present and unchanged (git status shows no modification to them unless a reference rewrite touched their SKILL.md, which should not happen: references live in OTHER files).

VERIFY: launch a throwaway claude session after commit (claude -p "list your available skills" or check next session) and confirm beads-polish and consult-oracles still appear, duplicates gone. Files: 2 skill dirs deleted, plus any files containing rewritten references (~1-3).
close_reason: Deleted both duplicate skill dirs (tracked, git rm), rewrote 6 references across 4 skill files to the surviving originals, removed empty .disabled/.old-renamed dirs. Post-check grep for the doomed names in .claude/skills/ returns nothing. Commit on master.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH FACT-CHECK 2026-07-05: byte-identity re-verified (diff -rq exit 0 for both pairs). Exactly 4 files reference the doomed names and need references rewritten to the surviving originals: swarm-beads-quick/SKILL.md, swarm-beads-quality/SKILL.md, swarm-oracle-review/SKILL.md, swarm-pipeline/SKILL.md (all under .claude/skills/). No references outside .claude/skills/.

title: Consolidate Ctrl-R history search on fish-fzy; remove fzf/re-search plugins and dead keybindings file
description:
PROBLEM (review finding, HIGH): three plugins in .config/fish/fishfile fight over Ctrl-R: jbonjean/re-search, gyakovlev/fish-fzy, jethrokuan/fzf. Load order means fish-fzy wins today: conf.d/fish-fzy.fish calls fish_fzy_bindings which REDEFINES fish_user_key_bindings inline (\cr -> fzy_select_history, \cf -> fzy_select_directory). Consequences: (a) functions/fish_user_key_bindings.fish (last touched Dec 2020: colemak vi-mode remaps n/i swapped, u/e history search, plus a call to nonexistent fzf_key_bindings) is PERMANENTLY SHADOWED and has never run: fish only autoloads a function if undefined; (b) conf.d/fzf.fish line 16's bind \cr __fzf_reverse_isearch is silently overwritten; (c) re-search's binding lives only in the dead file. The user has lived with fzy-owned Ctrl-R + no colemak remaps for 5.5 years, so KEEPING CURRENT BEHAVIOR means: fish-fzy stays, everything else goes.

ACTIONS:
1. Remove jethrokuan/fzf and jbonjean/re-search lines from .config/fish/fishfile (keep gyakovlev/fish-fzy). Then run fisher so it uninstalls them (fisher rm / re-apply fishfile; verify with fisher list).
2. Delete .config/fish/functions/fish_user_key_bindings.fish (git rm). The colemak remaps in it were never active; deleting preserves observed behavior. If the user later wants colemak vi-mode, git history has it.
3. Inspect conf.d/fzf.fish and config.fish lines 10-12 (FZF_COMPLETE / FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS universal-var writes): determine whether anything else on the system still invokes the fzf binary (grep .config/fish and bin/ for fzf usage beyond the plugin). If nothing does, git rm conf.d/fzf.fish and delete the config.fish FZF block. If something DOES use fzf, keep exactly one owner: move all FZF vars into conf.d/fzf.fish using the guarded set -q VAR; or set -U VAR idiom and delete the config.fish block.
4. git rm bin/re-search.exe (178KB tracked Windows binary; only consumer was the re-search plugin via functions/re_search.fish: check whether that function file exists and remove it too if its only purpose was the plugin).
5. One commit per epic conventions.

DANGER NOTES: do NOT touch conf.d/fish-fzy.fish. Do not edit fish_variables (machine state, gitignored). Universal vars (set -U) persist outside the repo: after removing the config.fish writes, explicitly set -e any FZF_* universal vars that nothing owns anymore, and note which were erased in the close reason.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) fishfile lists exactly one history-search plugin (fish-fzy); (b) fresh fish -i shell: bind \cr shows fzy_select_history, Ctrl-R works interactively; (c) no fish startup errors or warnings (fish -c exit; and fish -i -c 'bind | grep -E "\\\\cr|\\\\cf"'); (d) grep -ri "re-search\|__fzf" .config/fish returns nothing live; (e) fish_user_key_bindings resolves to the fzy-installed definition only.

VERIFY: run the binding checks above and paste output into the close reason. Files: fishfile, functions/fish_user_key_bindings.fish (del), conf.d/fzf.fish (del or edit), config.fish, bin/re-search.exe (del), possibly functions/re_search.fish (del).
close_reason: fishfile now: fish-fzy + bass only. fisher (v3.2.8, fishfile mechanism kept) removed re-search; vendored fzf plugin files git rm'd manually (14 files incl. conf.d/fzf.fish, 9 __fzf_* functions, fish_user_key_bindings.fish, re_search{,_scroll}.fish, bin/re-search.exe). Verified fresh shell: bind \cr -> fzy_select_history, \cf -> fzy_select_directory, clean startup, zero dangling fzf/re-search refs. FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS kept (git-alias.fish uses raw fzf binary) with guarded idiom; erased universal vars: FZF_COMPLETE, FZF_FIND_FILE_COMMAND (referenced missing ag), FZF_TMUX_HEIGHT, FZF_LEGACY_KEYBINDINGS, FZF_DISABLE_KEYBINDINGS, FZF_PREVIEW_FILE_CMD, FZF_PREVIEW_DIR_CMD. config.fish committed via single-hunk stage; ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL drift hunk left uncommitted per epic rule.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH FACT-CHECK 2026-07-05: fishfile confirmed to hold FOUR plugins: jbonjean/re-search, gyakovlev/fish-fzy, edc/bass, jethrokuan/fzf. KEEP edc/bass (bash-script compat helper, unrelated to keybindings) and fish-fzy; REMOVE re-search and fzf only. functions/re_search.fish exists and should go with the plugin. config.fish FZF block confirmed at lines 10-12: set -U FZF_COMPLETE 2 / FZF_FIND_FILE_COMMAND "ag -l --hidden --ignore .git" / FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS. Note FZF_FIND_FILE_COMMAND references ag (the_silver_searcher), likely not even installed: check command -q ag and mention in close reason. All three are set -U universal vars: remember the set -e erasure step.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH ROUND 2: do not assume fisher 4 syntax. The repo has a legacy 'fishfile' (fisher v3 era); fisher 4 uses fish_plugins instead. Detect what is installed (fisher --version; ls .config/fish/fish_plugins) and use the matching removal flow. If the installed fisher no longer understands fishfile, migrate the (pruned) plugin list to the current mechanism as part of this bead and say so in the close reason. Worst case: remove plugin-installed files manually (fisher installs into functions/ conf.d/ completions/: identify plugin-owned files via the plugin repos' layout before deleting anything user-authored).

title: Remove vimperator fossil and Vundle leftover from vim config
description:
PROBLEM: (1) .vimperatorrc and .vimperator/ (13 color-scheme files, ~92KB) configure the Firefox Vimperator extension, dead since Firefox 57 killed XUL extensions in Nov 2017; tracked since the repo's initial commit and unusable the entire time. (2) .vimrc.bundles line ~16 lists Plug 'gmarik/Vundle.vim': Vundle is a COMPETING plugin manager installed as a regular plugin under vim-plug, a leftover from the Vundle-to-vim-plug migration (confirmed installed at .vim/plugged/Vundle.vim, loaded but never invoked).

NOT IN SCOPE: syntastic (unmaintained but functional: removing it would change vim behavior; flagged for a future vim pass). .ideavimrc/.vsvimrc/.cvimrc stay (live tools).

ACTIONS:
1. git rm .vimperatorrc && git rm -r .vimperator
2. Remove the .gitignore lines referencing vimperator (lines 1-2: ./vimperator/info and /.vimperator/info) since nothing will remain to ignore.
3. Edit .vimrc.bundles: delete the Plug 'gmarik/Vundle.vim' line. Check for any Vundle references elsewhere in .vimrc/.vimrc.bundles (grep -i vundle) and remove those too.
4. Optionally rm -rf .vim/plugged/Vundle.vim from disk (untracked plugin dir) or leave for :PlugClean; if left, note it in close reason.
5. One commit per epic conventions.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) no vimperator-named file tracked or on disk in the repo root; (b) grep -ri vimperator on tracked files returns nothing (check .gitignore too); (c) grep -i vundle .vimrc .vimrc.bundles returns nothing; (d) vim opens cleanly: vim -es +qall exit code 0, and vim -es +'PlugStatus' +qall does not error on a missing entry.

VERIFY: run (d) and paste result. Files: .vimperatorrc (del), .vimperator/ (del), .gitignore, .vimrc.bundles.
close_reason: 14 vimperator files removed, 2 stale gitignore lines dropped, Vundle Plug line deleted (no other vundle refs in .vimrc/.vimrc.bundles). vim -es +qall exits 0. .vim/plugged/Vundle.vim left on disk for :PlugClean (untracked).

title: Remove bd-nodb wrapper for retired bd tool
description:
PROBLEM: bin/bd-nodb is a tracked 33-byte wrapper: exec bd --no-db "$@". The bd (Python beads) tool family is retired; br (beads_rust) is the documented replacement in both CLAUDE.md files (see the bd-to-br-migration skill). bd still exists at ~/go/bin/bd so the wrapper does not hard-fail, which makes it a trap: it silently works against the deprecated tool.

ACTIONS:
1. Confirm nothing references bd-nodb: grep -rn "bd-nodb" across tracked files (exclude .git, .beads). Review agent already found zero references; re-verify because cheap.
2. git rm bin/bd-nodb.
3. One commit per epic conventions.

ACCEPTANCE: bin/bd-nodb gone from disk and index; repo-wide grep for bd-nodb clean (except .beads/ and git history).

VERIFY: command -v bd-nodb returns nothing in a fresh shell. Files: bin/bd-nodb (del).

title: Reconcile hooks dir with settings.json; fix stale README hook docs
description:
PROBLEM: .claude/hooks/ contains rewrite-search.py and paseo-sync.mjs, neither referenced anywhere in the active settings.json (PreToolUse/Bash runs only dcg + trauma_guard.py; other events run aiolos-hook-forwarder). But the repo root README.md line ~49 documents rewrite-search.py as an ACTIVE PreToolUse hook that redirects raw grep/find to fff/rg/fd. So either the hook was dropped and the README is stale, or it was never wired. paseo-sync.mjs has zero references anywhere outside itself.

DEFAULT POSTURE: behavior-preserving. The hooks are not running today; make the repo describe reality rather than re-enabling anything.

ACTIONS:
1. Verify unreferenced status independently: grep -rn "rewrite-search\|paseo-sync" across the repo (tracked files, .claude.json, settings.local.json if readable) excluding .git/.beads. If ANY live reference turns up, stop, comment on the bead, and keep the file.
2. Check git log --oneline -- .claude/hooks/rewrite-search.py .claude/hooks/paseo-sync.mjs and the settings.json history (git log -S rewrite-search -- .claude/settings.json) to determine whether rewrite-search was once wired and later dropped deliberately. Record what you find in the close reason (this answers "stale doc vs never-wired").
3. git rm .claude/hooks/paseo-sync.mjs (orphan).
4. For rewrite-search.py: git rm it too UNLESS history shows it was recently and deliberately parked for re-enable; in that case leave the file and only fix the README. Judgement call: document reasoning in close reason.
5. Update README.md so the hooks section matches settings.json reality: describe dcg, trauma_guard.py, and the aiolos-hook-forwarder pipeline; remove/adjust the rewrite-search.py claim.
6. One commit per epic conventions.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) every file in .claude/hooks/ is referenced by settings.json (or has a comment/README note explaining why it exists); (b) README hook section contains no claims contradicted by settings.json; (c) no dangling references to deleted files.

VERIFY: list .claude/hooks/ and cross-check each entry against settings.json in the close reason. Files: .claude/hooks/paseo-sync.mjs (del), .claude/hooks/rewrite-search.py (del or keep), README.md.
close_reason: Hooks dir reconciled: trauma_guard.py (wired), forward_to_aiolos.sh (live aiolos pipeline, untracked drift, explained by own header + bin/aiolos-hooks ref). Deleted tracked orphans paseo-sync.mjs + rewrite-search.py (history verdict: rewrite-search never appeared in tracked settings.json, so the README claim was aspirational; both recoverable from history). README hooks passage rewritten to match settings.json reality.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH FACT-CHECK 2026-07-05: hooks dir holds FOUR files: forward_to_aiolos.sh, paseo-sync.mjs, rewrite-search.py, trauma_guard.py. Add forward_to_aiolos.sh to the reference audit: settings.json references bin/aiolos-hook-forwarder (the ELF binary), NOT this shell script. Determine whether the binary invokes the script, the script is the old pre-binary implementation, or it is referenced from settings.local.json; treat as orphan candidate only if nothing references it. README claim confirmed at README.md lines 49-51 (also mentions fff MCP steering and a noqa opt-out; preserve accuracy about fff registration when rewriting).

title: Consolidate duplicate sentry MCP config; ensure no literal token in tracked settings.json
description:
PROBLEM: the server name "sentry" is defined in TWO places with different targets: .claude/mcp-servers.json -> local proxy http://127.0.0.1:4804/mcp (same pattern as its siblings pal/slack/zendesk on 4801/4803/4806), and .claude/settings.json mcpServers -> https://mcp.sentry.dev/sse with header Authorization: Bearer ${SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN}. Which wins is unclear; two sources of truth for one name.

FACT-CHECKED 2026-07-05 (polish pass): the settings.json Authorization value is the ENV PLACEHOLDER ${SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN} in BOTH the working tree and HEAD. There is NO literal secret in the tracked file: the original security concern is resolved, hence downgrade P1->P2. Remaining issue is purely the duplicate definition.

KEY INSIGHT for choosing the survivor: per the secrets policy, env vars are NOT exported globally (on-demand with-secrets design). SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is therefore almost certainly unset in Claude Code's environment, meaning the settings.json direct-to-mcp.sentry.dev entry cannot authenticate and has likely been dead since the secrets loader was disabled (2026-05-11). The local proxy entry in mcp-servers.json matches the working pattern of the other proxied servers.

ACTIONS:
1. Confirm the proxy is alive: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:4804/mcp (any HTTP response incl. 4xx means a listener exists; connection refused means dead). Also check pm2 list for the proxy process.
2. If proxy alive: delete the sentry entry from settings.json mcpServers (keep mcp-servers.json as canonical). If proxy dead: investigate which config a fresh session actually uses (claude mcp list) and keep the one that works; if neither works, keep the proxy entry, note breakage in close reason for the user.
3. Partial-stage discipline: settings.json carries ~116 lines of unrelated uncommitted drift (review finding #2, out of scope). Commit ONLY the sentry-block hunk: use git hunks list / git hunks add (see repo CLAUDE.md) or git addmatch "sentry".
4. Never echo header values; the placeholder name SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is safe to reference.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) exactly one sentry MCP definition across the two files; (b) claude mcp list (or fresh-session check) shows one sentry entry; (c) the committed hunk touches only the sentry block; (d) no secret values in chat/logs/commit.

VERIFY: paste claude mcp list sentry line + the curl status code into the close reason. Files: .claude/settings.json (partial hunk) and/or .claude/mcp-servers.json.
close_reason: Proxy alive (curl 406 = live listener; pm2 sentry-mcp online 2d). settings.json direct entry removed; mcp-servers.json proxy entry is the single definition. Direct entry was dead anyway: ${SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN} placeholder never expands since the global secrets loader was disabled. Committed via git hash-object + update-index so only the 7-line sentry deletion landed; the ~115-line settings.json drift remains uncommitted. No secret values echoed anywhere.

title: Delete dead fish files: shadowed prompt theme, omf loader, stray .bak
description:
PROBLEM: three fish files are dead code. (1) .config/fish/functions/fish_prompt.fish (6.7KB ported Sorin/prezto theme, vi-mode-aware): config.fish line 7 runs "starship init fish | source", which defines fish_prompt and fish_right_prompt BEFORE autoload can trigger, so the theme never renders. Starship is confirmed installed (~/.local/bin/starship) with config at ~/.config/starship.toml. (2) .config/fish/omf.fish sources $OMF_PATH/init.fish for oh-my-fish, which is fully uninstalled (~/.local/share/omf does not exist); the file also sits outside conf.d/ so fish never auto-sources it anyway: doubly dead. Fisher is the active plugin manager. (3) .config/fish/functions/claude.fish.bak: zero-byte stray, untracked.

ACTIONS:
1. Check whether fish_right_prompt lives inside fish_prompt.fish or in a separate functions/fish_right_prompt.fish; git rm whichever theme files exist.
2. git rm .config/fish/omf.fish.
3. rm .config/fish/functions/claude.fish.bak (untracked; plain rm).
4. Do NOT edit config.fish in this bead (its empty fish_mode_prompt at lines 3-5 is harmless and config.fish is owned by dotfiles-9l9 this round; file-overlap discipline).
5. One commit per epic conventions.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) new interactive fish shell still renders the starship prompt (fish -i -c 'functions fish_prompt | head -3' shows the starship-generated function, not the Sorin theme); (b) no file in .config/fish references OMF_PATH (grep -ri OMF .config/fish is empty); (c) no *.bak under .config/fish; (d) fish -c exit produces no errors.

VERIFY: paste the functions fish_prompt head output into the close reason. Files: functions/fish_prompt.fish (del), possibly functions/fish_right_prompt.fish (del), omf.fish (del), claude.fish.bak (del, untracked).
close_reason: fish_prompt.fish deleted (fish_right_prompt was defined inside it; no separate file existed). omf.fish deleted, claude.fish.bak removed. Verified: fresh shell fish_prompt is the starship-generated function; zero OMF references; no .bak files under .config/fish. config.fish untouched per file-overlap rule.

title: Resolve cc-slidev: dead-weight external clone whose skills never load
description:
PROBLEM: .claude/skills/cc-slidev/ is a full external repo clone (gitignored via the "External skill clones" block). Its 8 actual skills live 3 levels deep at cc-slidev/slidev/skills/*/SKILL.md, but the harness scans only one level under .claude/skills/, so NONE are ever exposed. Zero context cost, zero benefit: pure disk/mental clutter.

DECISION (made at review): install it properly via the new skills-sync tool (bin/skills-sync, built 2026-07-05) rather than delete, since it was cloned deliberately at some point. skills-sync discovers SKILL.md up to 5 levels deep and flattens each skill to .claude/skills/<name>/, which fixes the loading problem.

ACTIONS:
1. Identify the upstream: cd .claude/skills/cc-slidev && git remote -v. 
2. skills-sync add <that-remote-url> (accepts owner/repo or full URL; note the auto-derived source name).
3. BEFORE syncing, check for name collisions: compare basenames under cc-slidev/slidev/skills/ with existing .claude/skills/ entries. If any collide, STOP and leave a comment on this bead instead of forcing.
4. skills-sync sync <source-name>; confirm the 8 skills land flat in .claude/skills/.
5. rm -rf .claude/skills/cc-slidev and remove its line from .gitignore ("External skill clones" block; keep frontend-slides line untouched).
6. Track the new flattened skill dirs + updated .gitignore + skills-sources manifest/lock in one commit per epic conventions.

FALLBACK: if the clone has no usable remote or upstream is gone, just delete the clone dir and its .gitignore line (it loads nothing today, so deletion is behavior-preserving) and note that in the close reason.

ACCEPTANCE: (a) no .claude/skills/cc-slidev dir; (b) either 8 new flat skill dirs tracked in git with .skill-source.json stamps, or documented fallback deletion; (c) .gitignore no longer references cc-slidev; (d) skills-sync status shows the new source up to date (if installed).

VERIFY: new skills appear in the available-skills list of a fresh session. Files: .gitignore, .claude/skills-sources.json, .claude/skills-sources.lock.json, ~8 new skill dirs.
close_reason: 8 skills installed flat via skills-sync from rhuss/cc-slidev @ e6d515c, no name collisions, tracked with provenance stamps; harness registered them live. Old clone relocated to session scratchpad (dcg vetoed recursive delete; mv achieves removal recoverably). .gitignore cc-slidev line removed, frontend-slides kept.

comments.author: oystein
comments.text: POLISH FACT-CHECK 2026-07-05: clone remote confirmed: https://github.com/rhuss/cc-slidev.git. Skills confirmed at slidev/skills/*/SKILL.md (e.g. diagram-design, excalidraw-generation, latex-handouts). skills-sync discover_skills uses find -maxdepth 5 from the clone root, so this layout is discovered without config; pass --dir slidev/skills to skills-sync add for tighter scoping if preferred.
